name: Pork Pinwheels with Roasted-Garlic Mayonnaise
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons snipped fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
1/4 cup grated Parmesan or Romano cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium skillet, cook leeks in the 1 tablespoon olive oil until tender. Remove from heat.
2. In a blender or food processor container, combine parsley, thyme, Parmesan or Romano cheese, salt, and pepper. Cover and blend on several on-off turns until finely chopped. With the machine running, gradually add the 3 tablespoons oil; blend until mixture is smooth with a butter-like consistency. Add leeks and nuts; process on several on-off turns until coarsely chopped. Set aside.
3. Remove fat and thin membranes from pork tenderloins. Using a sharp knife, cut each tenderloin lengthwise to within the opposite side but not through it. Cover one tenderloin with plastic wrap. Pound the tenderloin flat using the flat side of a meat mallet, working from the center to edges. Remove wrap and repeat with remaining tenderloin.
4. Sprinkle tenderloins lightly with additional salt and pepper. Spread half the parsley mixture evenly over each tenderloin, leaving at least 1 inch free of the edge. Roll up each tenderloin from a short side. Tie rolls with string if necessary. Insert a meat thermometer into the center of one tenderloin.
5. Place meat on a rack in a shallow roasting pan and roast uncovered in a 325°F oven for 45-60 minutes or until the thermometer reads 160°F. Let cool about 30 minutes at room temperature. Remove string, cut slices about 1/2 inch thick, cover, and chill. Serve with Roasted-Garlic Mayonnaise.
6. Roasted-Garlic Mayonnaise:
- Peel away outer skin from a medium head of garlic.
- Cut off the pointed top portion with a knife, leaving the bulb intact but exposing individual cloves.
- Place in a small baking dish and drizzle with 2 teaspoons olive oil.
- Bake, covered alongside the meat, for 45-60 minutes or until cloves are very soft. Let cool slightly.
- Press to remove garlic paste from individual cloves. Mash with tines of a fork. Stir together with half a cup light mayonnaise dressing. Thin with a little milk if necessary. Cover and chill until serving.
